subject: [PATCH 00/13] mm: PMD-level swap entries for anonymous THPs

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the implementation of PMD swap entries, specifically the conversion of swp_entry_to_pmd() callers to softleaf_to_pmd(). The author agreed that this refactoring is necessary to keep feature patches focused on new functionality and not mix it with existing code. A prep patch will be added to make these changes before introducing the new PMD swap entry support.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the swapoff path needing to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(). They moved the double-checked locking pattern into ensure_on_mmlist() in mm/internal.h and converted both callers, agreeing that this change is necessary for upcoming PMD-level swap entry code paths.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern that pagemap_pmd_range_thp() will crash on non-present PMD-level swap entries, explaining that it assumes every non-present PMD is a migration entry and unconditionally calls softleaf_to_page(). The author proposed adding a guard with softleaf_has_pfn(), matching how pte_to_pagemap_entry() handles non-present PTEs.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern that change_non_present_huge_pmd() would crash on mprotect() when encountering a PMD swap entry by moving the call to softleaf_to_folio() into the migration_write branch, ensuring it only uses the folio pointer in that specific case.
reviews.raw_body:
change_non_present_huge_pmd() calls softleaf_to_folio() unconditionally
at the top of the function.  softleaf_to_folio() extracts a PFN from
the entry and converts it to a folio pointer, which is only meaningful
for migration and device_private entries that encode a real PFN.

A swap entry encodes a swap offset instead, so softleaf_to_folio()
would produce a bogus pointer and crash on mprotect() when a PMD swap
entry is present.

Move the call into the migration_write branch where the folio is
actually used, so the function is safe for any non-present PMD type.

Signed-off-by: Usama Arif <usama.arif@linux.dev>
---
 mm/huge_memory.c | 3 ++-
 1 file changed, 2 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/mm/huge_memory.c b/mm/huge_memory.c
index 49da0746b8ca..d82a19b5e276 100644
--- a/mm/huge_memory.c
+++ b/mm/huge_memory.c
@@ -2619,11 +2619,12 @@ static void change_non_present_huge_pmd(struct mm_struct *mm,
 		bool uffd_wp_resolve)
 {
 	softleaf_t entry = softleaf_from_pmd(*pmd);
-	const struct folio *folio = softleaf_to_folio(entry);
 	pmd_t newpmd;
 
 	VM_WARN_ON(!pmd_is_valid_softleaf(*pmd));
 	if (softleaf_is_migration_write(entry)) {
+		const struct folio *folio = softleaf_to_folio(entry);
+
 		/*
 		 * A protection check is difficult so
 		 * just be safe and disable write
-- 
2.52.0

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the softleaf layer not recognizing PMD swap entries by adding new helpers to each architecture that supports THP migration, mirroring existing PTE swap exclusive helpers.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the swap branch in __split_huge_pmd_locked() not handling PMD swap entries correctly, by adding a new branch that splits a PMD swap entry into 512 PTE swap entries without needing a folio reference. The author also added checks to ensure that page and folio are left uninitialized in this branch, and that any future caller breaking the invariant is caught by VM_WARN_ON_ONCE(freeze).

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about handling PMD swap entries during fork and copy operations by teaching copy_huge_pmd() and copy_huge_non_present_pmd() to duplicate the swap references instead of splitting them, mirroring the existing behavior for PTEs. The author also added retry logic in case of allocation failures. A fix is planned.
sentiment_signals: added new code to address the issue

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about how PMD-level swap entries are handled during swapoff, specifically the need to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(). The author agreed to restructure in v2 and added code to unuse_pmd() and call it from unuse_pmd_range() to swap in PMD-level swap entries as whole THPs.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed the concern that non-present PMD walkers needed to be taught about swap entries by mirroring the PTE-level equivalents. The author added new helpers and modified existing functions in various files to handle PMD swap entries, including smaps_account_swap(), zap_huge_pmd(), change_non_present_huge_pmd(), move_soft_dirty_pmd(), clear_soft_dirty_pmd(), make_uffd_wp_pmd(), pagemap_pmd_range_thp(), and madvise_free_huge_pmd(). The author also modified hmm_vma_handle_absent_pmd() to fault in PMD swap entries via hmm_vma_fault().

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a mistake in the patch series, specifically forgetting to add Hugh to the CC list, and apologized for it.
reviews.raw_body:
+Hugh. Hugh raised this in [1], and I completely forgot to add him
to the series, sorry about that!

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/all/6869b7f0-84e1-fb93-03f1-9442cdfe476b@google.com/

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Zi Yan pointed out that the patch series is not properly ordered in git history to preserve bisectability, citing a specific link to an older discussion about THP swap work.

Reviewer Zi Yan questioned how the patch handles insufficient memory during swap-in, suggesting that splitting should be straightforward since there's a contiguous swap space at swap-out time.

Reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where a PMD swap entry is split into PTEs due to zswap or non-contiguous swap allocation, and requested the addition of fallback logic
reviews.raw_body:
+Ying, who did the original THP swap work[1].

[1] https://lkml.org/lkml/2016/8/9/588

On 27 Apr 2026, at 6:01, Usama Arif wrote:

---

I was wondering how to handle insufficient memory during swap-in.
Here it is. I have not read the code, but the split should be
straightforward, since we already have a contiguous swap space at
swap-out time and the split is just to enable PTE-level swap in, right?

---

Best Regards,
Yan, Zi

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that they should have CC'd Zi Yan earlier and added more reviewers to the patch series, but did not address any specific technical concerns or indicate a need for further revision.

Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreed to restructure in v2.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks Zi!

Sorry Ying for not CCing you! checkpatch on the whole series produced
a really long list and I wasnt sure if people would start thinking of
it as spam. I added reviewers and maintainers of swap and THP + a few
folks that commented on previous related work from which this kicked off.
I should have just CC'ed everyone.

---

Yes that is correct. Actually patch 6 was one of the easier patches.
If the kernel can't allocate 2M, memcg charge fails and a few other reasons,
we split THP.

2026-04-27.patch_summary: This series of patches introduces PMD-level swap entries for anonymous THPs, allowing huge mappings to be preserved across the swap round-trip and reducing memory usage when swapping. The existing PTE-split path is still used as a fallback in cases where PMD-order resources are unavailable. This change has benefits including faster fault handling on swap-in, reduced memory allocation during swap, and improved performance for walkers such as zap and mprotect.

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: Reviewer David (Arm) expressed no specific technical concerns or objections, but mentioned that he would review the patch soon after being slowed down by LSF/MM and travel commitments.
reviews.raw_body:
All sounds nice. I'll get to review this soon. LSF/MM and travel will slow me a
bit down in May :(

-- 
Cheers,

David

The existing PTE-split path is still used as a fallback in cases where PMD-order resources are unavailable. This change has benefits including faster fault handling on swap-in, reduced memory allocation during swap, and improved performance for walkers such as zap and mprotect.
